$schema: Hand-edited. Read by update-status.mjs and merged into live-status.json under `summary.productionReadiness`. Render in live-status.html → renderProductionReadiness().
_updateWorkflow: When a stage's status changes, update the matching stage entry in this file.
Append a row to `history` with { date: 'YYYY-MM-DD', stage, from, to, note, commit?: 'sha or PR url' }.
Keep `baseline` IMMUTABLE — that field captures the pre-work state on 2026-05-24. Only `current` and `history` evolve.
Run `node docs/status/update-status.mjs` to push the change through the cron pipeline.
DO NOT delete history entries — the dashboard renders them as the audit trail.

tracks.name: Voice / Digital Assistant
tracks.description: Production-readiness for the conversational voice agent (Wave E + Wave D). Spans intent coverage, eval regression suite, PHI safety, telephony backends, pilot ops, and runtime monitoring.

stages.name: Stage 1 — Capability completeness
stages.rationale: Every conversational intent (book / cancel / reschedule / confirm / list / pay / verify-insurance / transfer / end) has an end-to-end implementation + STRIDE adversarial test.

baseline.summary: 12 intents shipped end-to-end. 741+ tests across packages/voice-agent; 74% line / 87% branch coverage. STRIDE adversarial cases (prompt injection, identity bypass, cross-tenant leakage) covered.

current.summary: Unchanged. 12 intents, 741+ tests, 74% / 87% coverage.

stages.name: Stage 2 — Eval harness
stages.rationale: Regression suite of scripted golden conversations + scorers. Lets us change prompts / intents / state machine without holding our breath. Runs on every PR.

baseline.summary: No `evals/` or `golden-transcripts/` directory existed. Only `improvement/transcript-reviewer.ts` ran post-call heuristics — no pre-deployment gate, no per-intent SLA tracking, no hallucination detector.

current.summary: @cernia/voice-agent-evals package shipped. 8 goldens covering book-appointment, lookup-practitioner, transfer-to-human, PHI gate + verification dance (3-turn DOB/2FA), red-flag chest-pain short-circuit, unsupported-language transfer, cost-ceiling trip. 3 scorers (intent-emission, agent-text expectations, PHI-leak regex). 97 ms wall clock. Wired into CI via `pnpm --filter @cernia/voice-agent-evals test`. SHIPPED 2026-05-25: .github/workflows/voice-agent-evals.yml runs the 8-golden suite per-commit on push to main + every PR touching packages/voice-agent/** or packages/voice-agent-evals/**. Workflow-dispatch trigger for manual runs. ~100ms wall clock, no API keys required (deterministic scripted-claude fixture). Catches model-prompt regressions, intent-router drift, PHI-leak regressions, red-flag screener failures BEFORE merge.

stages.rationale: PHI redaction in every log sink, audit emission per dialog turn, BAA-covered provider allowlist, output-side clinical-content classifier, caller rate-limiting.

tracks.name: Functionality Utilization (cross-package)
tracks.description: Audit + remediation of packages built but not wired into apps/*. The 2026-05-24 shelf-ware audit found 286 total packages, 36 imported by apps/*/src, ~140 lazy-load-by-design (catalog-* and specialty-* — loaded on-demand per clinical workflow, NOT shelf-ware), and ~30-40 genuine infrastructure built without app wiring. Each stage groups one coherent cluster of unwired infra; current=absent means no app instantiates any package in the cluster.

stages.name: CDS Hooks & quality measures
stages.rationale: FHIR CDS Hooks is the standard for surfacing clinical decision support inside the EMR workflow. CQM is the Centers for Medicare quality-measure compute engine. Both are core regulatory/clinical infra that have to run server-side for the platform to qualify for value-based-care reimbursement.

baseline.summary: packages/cds-hooks and packages/cqm-engine exist with full unit tests; ZERO importers in apps/*/src. No CDS Hook endpoint mounted in apps/api. No CQM batch job. Quality measure scores can't surface in any UI.

current.summary: CDS Hooks runtime wired into apps/api. GET /cds-services discovery + POST /cds-services/:hookId invocation are live, backed by @cernia/cds-hooks's InMemoryCdsServiceRegistry + HookRouter + HookInvocationHandler. One starter `patient-view` service ships a `voice-agent available` info card. 6 new integration tests cover discovery, happy-path invocation, unknown hookId 404, invalid body 400, inactive-service filtering. apps/api: 1894 tests pass (was 1888). CQM batch + admin UI remain — see remaining.

remaining: POST /admin/workflows/:id/$run + POST /admin/bots/:id/$run kickoff routes SHIPPED (apps/api/src/admin-cds-dispatch-routes.ts, registered at apps/api/src/index.ts §11d.quater, 6 unit tests). Routes return 202 with {dispatchId, queuedAt, mode: 'catalog-acknowledged', hadLaunchToken, kind, hookInstance}. Honest stub-followup: the landing pages do NOT yet call them on mount — that wiring is the next slice. AND the bot-runtime still operates in catalog-only mode (real per-tenant fire gated on the tenant_bot_instances table, tracked under bot-workflow-runtime stage). When tenant_bot_instances ships, the route's response mode will flip from 'catalog-acknowledged' to 'queued-for-tenant-runtime'.

DONE 2026-05-30 — cryptographic EHR-JWT signature verification WIRED into apps/api. The crypto verifier (verifyEhrJwtAsync — jose.jwtVerify against the EHR's JWKS, TTL cache, jti single-use, alg allow-list, fail-closed) already existed in @cernia/cds-hooks; the missing apps/api piece is now shipped: apps/api/src/cds-ehr-jwks-resolver.ts (buildEhrIssuerResolver — iss→jwks_uri via explicit CERNIA_CDS_ISSUER_JWKS override OR SMART/OIDC .well-known discovery, https-only, unknown-issuer→null; buildInboundCdsJwtVerifier — composes the resolver + verifier + Bearer extraction). Wired in buildCdsHooksRuntime(): when CERNIA_CDS_JWT_VERIFICATION is enabled it builds the inbound verifier and turns the handler's structural-only sync check OFF (one stronger gate); the POST /cds-services/:hookId route pre-verifies the inbound JWT and returns 401 fail-closed on any failure. Opt-in / OFF by default → zero regression for existing deploys. 13 adversarial tests (valid / tampered-sig / unknown-issuer / expired / disallowed-alg / replay / missing-Bearer / resolver fail-closed / https-enforcement) against a local JWKS server + jose-signed JWTs. apps/api typecheck=0, build clean, resolver lint clean. Operational note: end-to-end activation still needs a real EHR partner publishing a reachable JWKS endpoint + an operator setting CERNIA_CDS_TRUSTED_ISSUERS — the CODE path is complete + tested.

history.note: Cryptographic EHR-JWT (JWKS) verification wired into apps/api (ra-cds-6 crypto followup). ra-cds-1 closed — registry is no longer process-global. New tenant-isolation route test asserts tenant-A registration is invisible to tenant-B discovery. apps/api typecheck clean; cds-hooks 218/218 tests green; cds-hooks-routes 7/7 green (also fixed a pre-existing baseline assertion that asserted exact services-array length).

Shipped ra-cds-2 RealDecisionDispatcher. apps/api/src/cds-decision-dispatcher.ts implements all three CdsServiceDecisionCallback kinds: (a) inline-fhirpath evaluates the FHIRPath expression via @cernia/core evaluatePath against {context, prefetch, hookInstance, hook}; truthy result emits a card built from cardTemplate with {fhirpath-expression} placeholders interpolated as sub-expressions against the same scope; falsy result or malformed expression emits no cards (degrades gracefully — never 500 to the EHR). (b) workflow kind emits an app-link SMART card pointing at <CERNIA_ADMIN_BASE_URL>/admin/workflows/<workflowRef.id>/run?hookInstance=<hi> per the v2.0 §1.2 long-running-logic rule. (c) bot kind emits the same shape pointing at /admin/bots/<botRef.id>/run. Composes with StarterCardDispatcher via {coversHookId, dispatch} delegate so the seeded handoff services preserve their hardcoded info-card UX while tenant-registered services exercise the real path. apps/api/src/cds-hooks-routes.ts buildCdsHooksRuntime() now wires the composite dispatcher with appBaseUrl sourced from CERNIA_ADMIN_BASE_URL env (default http://localhost:3000). 14 new unit tests pass + existing 7 cds-hooks-routes tests still pass — the starter UX is preserved. Per ADR 0033 the inline-fhirpath path runs synchronously through the in-repo FHIRPath evaluator (fast-path for pure dot-notation; AST for complex expressions) — well within the 500ms p95 budget. Per ADR 0004 the dispatcher has no tenant awareness; the per-tenant CdsService comes from the multi-tenant registry the router already scoped.

Shipped ra-cds-6 EHR-issuer trust-list scaffolding. apps/api/src/cds-jwt-config.ts ships buildCdsJwtRuntimeConfig() — env-driven loader for CERNIA_CDS_{JWT_VERIFICATION,TRUSTED_ISSUERS,EXPECTED_AUDIENCE,MAX_AGE_SECONDS,SKEW_SECONDS,ALLOWED_ALGORITHMS}. Defaults: verification disabled (preserves pilot-EHR-friendly behaviour). Fail-closed checks: CERNIA_CDS_JWT_VERIFICATION=true with an empty CERNIA_CDS_TRUSTED_ISSUERS is rejected at boot with CdsJwtConfigError (would otherwise accept JWTs from any issuer); CERNIA_CDS_EXPECTED_AUDIENCE must be explicitly set OR derivable from CERNIA_PUBLIC_BASE_URL+/cds-services (otherwise the aud claim cannot be checked and any service-targeted JWT would pass). apps/api/src/cds-hooks-routes.ts buildCdsHooksRuntime() accepts an optional `jwt:` config (defaults to env-derived) and wires it through every per-tenant HookInvocationHandler — handlers now run with {jwtVerificationEnabled, jwt} from the runtime config rather than the previously hardcoded {jwtVerificationEnabled: false}. Existing pilot-EHR deployments without CERNIA_CDS_* env keep the same behaviour. 10 new unit tests pass (apps/api/src/cds-jwt-config.test.ts) + 7 existing cds-hooks-routes tests still pass. The only thing now gated on a real EHR partner is populating CERNIA_CDS_TRUSTED_ISSUERS + the followup JWKS fetcher that does cryptographic signature verification.

(1) Mount the other standard hooks (order-select / order-sign / medication-prescribe / encounter-discharge) - shipped at apps/api/src/cds-hooks-routes.ts:159-184 (buildStarterClinicalWorkflowServices registers all 4 as info-card handoff services in the starter catalogue, task #100). (2) Schedule a nightly CQM batch job that emits MeasureReport resources per tenant - shipped at apps/api/src/saas-nightly-batches.ts:89 (task #216 ra-cds-4). Iter 88 — SMART app-launch landing pages shipped. apps/admin/src/app/admin/workflows/[id]/run/page.tsx + apps/admin/src/app/admin/bots/[id]/run/page.tsx now exist. They parse the SMART launch query params (hookInstance, fhirServer, launch, smartContext) and render a 'Dispatch received' state with a context dump + back-link to the workflow/bot detail. This retires the 404 a clinician got when clicking an app-link CDS card emitted by apps/api/src/cds-decision-dispatcher.ts. Honest scope boundary: neither page POSTs a kickoff to the bot-runtime — that still rides on the bot-workflow-runtime stage's tenant_bot_instances followup. Status: ra-cds-2-followup bullet narrowed (the 404 is closed; what remains is the actual workflow/bot dispatch wire to apps/api). apps/admin typecheck clean for both new files.

Iter 96 — backend SMART dispatch routes shipped. apps/api/src/admin-cds-dispatch-routes.ts mounts POST /admin/workflows/:id/$run + POST /admin/bots/:id/$run. Each: RBAC-gated (tenant-admin or office-admin only) via extractPrincipal mirroring admin-tenant-metrics-routes pattern; validates hookInstance is non-empty (400 otherwise); synthesizes a dispatchId from the hookInstance + a 1s-bucketed timestamp + 4B of CSPRNG salt so EHR replays within the same second get the same id (idempotent UX) but a fresh launch a second later gets a new id. Returns 202 with {dispatchId, queuedAt, mode: 'catalog-acknowledged', hadLaunchToken: boolean, kind: 'workflow'|'bot', hookInstance}. The `mode: 'catalog-acknowledged'` is the HONEST signal that downstream execution didn't fire — the bot-runtime is still in catalog-only mode. When tenant_bot_instances ships, the mode flips to 'queued-for-tenant-runtime'. 6 unit tests cover 401 no-principal, 403 non-admin, 400 missing-hookInstance, 202 happy-path with dispatchId pattern match, 202 with kind=bot + hadLaunchToken=false, response-never-echoes-SMART-launch-token. PHI posture: launch token never logged or returned; hookInstance is opaque correlation id. apps/api/src/index.ts §11d.quater wires the registration. The bullet remains in `remaining` because the apps/admin landing pages still need to call these routes on mount + the bot-runtime needs tenant_bot_instances for real execution; iter 96 closes ~half of the gap that was here.

stages.name: Caregiver delegation & identity federation
stages.rationale: Allows a patient's family/caregiver to act on their behalf (proxy), JIT-grant temporary access to a covering provider, SAML/OIDC federation for hospital-network SSO, and break-glass emergency access. Compliance-load-bearing for any enterprise pilot.

baseline.summary: g-d1-caregiver-proxy, g-d2-delegation, g-d3-jit-access, g-d4-access-review-attestation, g-d5-saml-oidc-federation, g-d6-break-glass all shipped. Existing auth flow doesn't consult any of them; no SAML/OIDC providers configured in apps/api boot path.

current.summary: Caregiver-federation backend complete, and BOTH SSO protocols (OIDC + SAML 2.0) are now wired end-to-end at apps/api boot with full session mint. OIDC: TenantSsoFactory (index.ts step 10i.1) with KmsBackedJwtIssuer + FHIR-User provisioner/lookup + Redis/InMemory state store + FetchOidcHttpClient + tenant-secret ClientSecretResolver. SAML 2.0: TenantSamlProviderFactory (index.ts step 10i.2) builds the hardened in-repo SamlIdentityProvider per tenant — real XML-DSig with XSW guards + RSA/ECDSA-SHA2 algorithm allowlist + replay cache + InResponseTo binding, JIT provisioning, role resolution, and session mint via the SAME jwtIssuer.sign path OIDC uses (byte-identical sessions). Routes: GET /metadata, GET /login (SP-initiated), POST /acs (full session). The earlier node-saml validator detour was removed in favour of this single canonical path. SSO routes still 503 for tenants with no enabled IdP connection, so wiring only widens what works. No SAML handshake work remains deferred (multi-pod InResponseTo store shares the OidcStateStore horizontal-scale follow-up; IdP-initiated SSO is a per-connection opt-in).

history.note: SAML 2.0 handshake CLOSED end-to-end — pivoted from the node-saml verifier detour to the hardened in-repo SamlIdentityProvider (@cernia/auth-providers), which the codebase already shipped as the canonical SAML SP. Discovery: SamlIdentityProvider.handleCallback already does the WHOLE handshake (real XML-DSig with XSW guards + RSA/ECDSA-SHA2 algorithm allowlist + replay cache + InResponseTo binding → JIT-provision → resolveRoles → jwtIssuer.sign → AuthenticateResult) — identical to the OIDC path. New apps/api/src/saml-provider-factory.ts (TenantSamlProviderFactory) maps the g-d5 SamlConnection → SamlProviderConfig (inverting attributeMapping) and constructs/caches one provider per (tenantId, connectionId) built with the boot jwtIssuer + a SAML FHIR-User store. saml-routes.ts rewritten: GET /metadata (provider.publishMetadata), GET /login (SP-initiated AuthnRequest, records the request id), POST /acs (provider.handleCallback → real Cernia session {token,sessionId,expiresAt,user}, 401/403 mapping). DELETED the redundant node-saml saml-validator.ts + saml-validator.test.ts and dropped @node-saml/node-saml + xml-crypto from apps/api. Bugfix: SamlIdentityProvider records JIT users with authProvider 'oauth2', so FhirUserProvisioner gained an externalAuthProvider option (default 'oidc') and the SAML user store is built with 'oauth2' — otherwise findByExternal (oidc-keyed) would re-provision a duplicate every login. Tests: saml-routes.test.ts rewritten (7) — drives the full SP-initiated flow (login→inflate AuthnRequest id→signed Response via the package's byte-exact _testBuildSignedSamlResponse against a runtime RSA keypair, no committed secrets→ACS), asserts JIT-provision + session mint + idpType='saml' + least-privilege defaultRoles fallback, plus 503/404/tamper-401/unsolicited-401/missing-400; sso-user-store.test.ts +1 (oauth2 re-login finds the user, oidc store does not). ADR-0127: @cernia/api typecheck=0, lint=0, build=0; @cernia/auth-providers rebuilt to expose the test helper. This CLOSES the long-deferred SAML last mile — SAML and OIDC now mint byte-identical sessions through one issuer.

[superseded by the pivot above] SAML 2.0 SP routes wired into apps/api (ACS verification slice). apps/api/src/saml-routes.ts: GET /api/auth/saml/:tenantId/metadata (SP metadata) + POST /api/auth/saml/:tenantId/acs (Assertion Consumer Service). The ACS handler reads the form-POSTed SAMLResponse (a urlencoded content-type parser is registered in the plugin scope), resolves the tenant's enabled SAML connection from the same PostgresIdpConnectionStore the OIDC factory uses, and runs it through buildTenantSamlValidator (real node-saml XML-DSig). 503 when the store isn't wired / 404 when no enabled SAML connection — safe-by-default, mirroring ssoRoutes. Registered at index.ts step 10i.2 (SP entity id from CERNIA_PUBLIC_BASE_URL, override CERNIA_SAML_SP_ENTITY_ID). 6 route tests (apps/api/src/saml-routes.test.ts) via app.inject: 503 (no store), 404 (no connection), SP-metadata XML, ACS accepts a runtime-signed Response and returns verified claims, ACS 401 on a tampered Response, ACS 400 on missing SAMLResponse — the happy path signs a real fixture with xml-crypto against a runtime openssl keypair (no committed secrets). ADR-0127: @cernia/api typecheck=0, new-file lint=0, build=0, 6/6 tests. The ACS endpoint returns {outcome:'verified', sessionPending:true}; the verified-claims → JIT-provision → session-mint bridge is the remaining last-mile (see deferred) — left for a dedicated slice so the SAML session is minted via the EXACT OIDC JwtIssuer path (no divergent hand-rolled session).

SAML 2.0 real XML-DSig verification SHIPPED — closes the cryptographic core of the long-deferred handshake (library = @node-saml/node-saml, operator-chosen). apps/api/src/saml-validator.ts: buildTenantSamlValidator(connection, opts) wraps node-saml SAML.validatePostResponseAsync for authoritative validation — XML signature (via xml-crypto), NotBefore/NotOnOrAfter, AudienceRestriction, StatusCode — plus a DEFENSE-IN-DEPTH explicit Issuer pin (node-saml's signature trust is certificate-based, so it accepts a validly-signed response regardless of the Issuer string; we additionally require profile.issuer === connection.issuer). Fail-closed: validatePostResponse throws on any failure → {ok:false}; claims never surface from an unverified response (ADR 0026 §3). wantAssertionsSigned=true. Maps node-saml Profile → typed SamlValidatedClaims (nameId, email, flattened attributes); SP metadata via generateServiceProviderMetadata. Tests (apps/api/src/saml-validator.test.ts) are REAL not mocked: generate a throwaway RSA keypair at RUNTIME (openssl; no committed secrets — repo gitignores *.pem) and sign a SAML Response with xml-crypto, then assert the validator ACCEPTS the valid signature + extracts claims and REJECTS tampered-payload / expired-Conditions / wrong-Audience / wrong-Issuer / unsigned (7/7). Deliberately NO stub verifier — that is exactly the bug class just fixed in the Stripe webhook path. Deps @node-saml/node-saml@^5.1.0 + xml-crypto@^6.1.2 added to apps/api. ADR-0127: @cernia/api typecheck=0, new-file lint=0, build=0, 7/7 tests. Remaining (now narrowed in `deferred`): the Fastify ACS route + boot wiring + JIT claim mapping.

Shipped PostgresProxyStore (apps/api/src/caregiver-proxy-store.ts) + migration 049_caregiver_proxy_relationships. Implements ProxyStore from @cernia/g-d1-caregiver-proxy with tenant-scoped CRUD: grant, revoke (legal-kind admin-only enforcement preserved), findById, listForCaregiver, listForPatient, sweepExpired (CTE-based bulk flip of active rows whose expires_at is past). 12 pg-mem unit tests cover happy path, kind-specific default expiry, legal-kind admin requirement, double-revoke protection, tenant isolation across all reads, sweepExpired CTE correctness. JIT request store + break-glass invocation log remain on InMemory (separate stores, same refactor pattern).

Caught + closed wiring gap: caregiverFederationRoutes was constructing a fresh InMemoryProxyStore (the PostgresProxyStore shipped earlier was sitting unused). Refactored buildCaregiverFederationRuntime to accept opts.proxyStore + apps/api boot now passes new PostgresProxyStore({ db, tenantId: CERNIA_DEFAULT_TENANT_ID ?? "platform" }). Routes are now backed by persistent storage.

Shipped JIT approve/deny + break-glass review endpoints with persistent stores. Migration 050 (jit_access_requests + break_glass_invocations). PostgresJitAccessStore + PostgresBreakGlassStore in apps/api with full tenant scoping. Routes updated: $request + $invoke now persist on ok results; new POST /access/jit/:id/$approve, /$deny, /access/break-glass/:id/$review; new GET /access/jit/pending + /access/break-glass/pending-review for the future admin UI. 7 pg-mem unit tests cover round-trip persistence across the engine state machines (request→approve, request→deny, invoke→review, listing filters, tenant isolation).

Shipped g-d2 user-to-user delegation end-to-end. Migration 051_access_delegations + PostgresAccessDelegationStore + routes mounted in caregiver-federation-routes: POST /access/delegation/$create + /:id/$accept + /:id/$reject + /:id/$revoke, GET /by-delegator/:id + /by-delegatee/:id. Engine state-machine guard rails preserved (only valid transitions persist; 409 invalid-state otherwise). 503 not-configured fallback when store unset. Wired in apps/api boot.

Shipped g-d4 access-review attestation end-to-end. Migration 052 (review_campaigns + attestation_items, two tables). PostgresAttestationStore handles both. 7 new routes: POST /access/attestation/campaigns/$create + /:id/items/$add + /:id/$close, POST /access/attestation/items/:id/$decide, GET /campaigns/:id/summary + /campaigns/:id/items, GET /items/by-reviewer/:id. All engine state-machine guard rails preserved (only-open campaigns accept new items; only-pending items accept decisions; closeCampaign emits itemsToRevoke for the unreviewed sweep).

Shipped admin UI for delegations + attestations. apps/admin/src/lib/caregiver-federation-api.ts (typed client) + 2 new pages: /admin/access/delegations (list by delegator OR delegatee, create form, accept/reject/revoke actions, state-machine-aware buttons) + /admin/access/attestations (campaign create form, per-reviewer pending queue, attest/revoke decisions with optional note). Sidebar gets a new "Access" nav group with both entries. The 17 caregiver-federation endpoints shipped earlier are now reachable from the tenant admin UI.

Shipped g-d5 IdP connection config layer: migration 053_idp_connections (per-tenant SAML 2.0 + OIDC configs, protocol-discriminated columns, JSONB attribute/group mappings), PostgresIdpConnectionStore (multi-tenant; tenantId per call). Tenant-admin CRUD routes mounted: GET/POST /admin/idp-connections, GET/PATCH/DELETE /admin/idp-connections/:id. Validation runs g-d5 engine.validateConnection() on insert; 422 with structured ConfigIssue list on failure. Admin role required for write; all reads tenant-scoped. 11 pg-mem tests cover OIDC + SAML round-trip, validation rejections, tenant isolation across every read/write, idempotent delete. The SAML/OIDC HANDSHAKE itself (AuthnRequest signing, SAMLResponse verification, ACS callback, session bridge) is the remaining piece — needs samlify/passport-saml in @cernia/auth-providers + login route changes.

Shipped TenantSsoFactory (apps/api/src/sso-tenant-factory.ts) — closes the g-d5 → auth-providers translation layer. Per-tenant OidcIdentityProvider construction on demand: reads active OIDC connection from PostgresIdpConnectionStore, resolves clientSecretRef via injected ClientSecretResolver (TenantSecretResolver in prod), maps g-d5 AttributeMapping → auth-providers claimMapping, strips .well-known suffix from discoveryUrl to derive issuer. Built-in cache keyed by (tenantId, connectionId); invalidate(tenantId) drops on admin toggle. 10 pg-mem tests cover happy path, no-enabled error, multi-enabled disambiguation, SAML-rejection, cache hit, cache invalidation, client-secret resolution, issuer derivation, attribute-mapping translation. Remaining for full handshake closure: Fastify routes wiring start + callback into Cernia URL space (needs JwtIssuer + UserProvisioner + persistent OidcStateStore wired in apps/api boot); SAML protocol implementation (needs samlify/passport-saml).

Shipped final piece of g-d5 OIDC chain: ssoRoutes (apps/api/src/sso-routes.ts) mounting GET /api/auth/sso/:tenantId/start + /callback. Routes default to 503 sso-not-configured until apps/api boot wires JwtIssuer + UserProvisioner + UserLookup + OidcStateStore + OidcHttpClient + ClientSecretResolver (clean stub in index.ts with explicit TODO inventory). 6 route tests cover the 503 path + a working happy path via a stub factory (302 redirect on /start, 200 authenticated payload with trusted user fields on /callback, 400 on idp-rejected / invalid-callback). Total caregiver-federation tests now 46 passing across 5 suites.

Wired the OIDC SSO route deps at apps/api boot — closed the last actionable caregiver-federation deferral (the SSO-boot-wiring bullet). New apps/api/src/sso-user-store.ts ships FhirUserProvisioner + FhirUserLookup over the same tenant-scoped StorageAdapter the FHIR server uses: create() JIT-provisions a FHIR User (authProvider=oidc, status=active), findByExternal() dedups by externalIdentifier (paged auth-provider search + in-memory filter — User has no external-identifier search param yet; dedicated param is a future optimization), resolveRoles() reads active RoleBindings→Role names mirroring iam-route-guards' resolver, loadMfaCredentials() returns null (IdP owns MFA). Safe-by-default: JIT users get NO RoleBindings (least privilege); a shared bounded userId→tenantId index recovers tenant for the tenant-less resolveRoles call (always preceded by find/create in the OIDC flow). index.ts step 10i.1 now builds TenantSsoFactory with KmsBackedJwtIssuer + RedisOidcStateStore (REDIS_HOST-gated, else InMemory) + FetchOidcHttpClient + ClientSecretResolver over tenantSecrets.resolver (connection clientSecretRef doubles as the secret provider key) + the new provisioner/lookup; SSO registration moved after the tenant-secret runtime so the resolver is available. Routes still 503 for tenants with no enabled IdP connection — wiring only widens what works. 13 new adapter unit tests; existing 6 sso-route tests still green. @cernia/api typecheck + build clean. Remaining: SAML 2.0 handshake only.

stages.name: SaaS billing & marketplace
stages.rationale: Enterprise invoicing, ASC 606 revenue recognition, SaaS metrics (MRR/ARR/churn), contract auto-renewal, discount-approval workflow, marketplace checkout, tax compliance. The commercial back-end for selling Cernia at scale.

No marketplace UI in apps/*. No invoice generation job. No tax engine wired into payment paths.

Bug surfaced by the 3b smoke test: packages/billing/src/backends/stripe-backend.ts defaultHmac used CommonJS require('node:crypto'), but @cernia/billing is type:module (ESM) → 'ReferenceError: require is not defined' on EVERY inbound POST /billing/webhook/stripe in production (apps/api constructs StripeBillingBackend with no injected hmac; all prior unit tests injected a mock hmac, masking it). Fix: top-level import { createHmac } from 'node:crypto'. Added 2 regression tests in stripe-backend.test.ts exercising the REAL defaultHmac (no injected hmac): valid sig accepted, tampered rejected. tracks.name: External Integrations & per-tenant config
tracks.description: Per-tenant credentials + vendor backends the voice agent talks to (Twilio for telephony, Deepgram for STT, ElevenLabs for TTS, OpenAI/Anthropic for LLM). Also covers self-serve tenant provisioning + per-tenant workflow customization. Without this layer every tenant shares the platform's trial credentials and can't customize the assistant.

stages.name: Tenant secrets — per-tenant credential storage
stages.rationale: Each tenant must paste their own Twilio/Deepgram/ElevenLabs/LLM credentials so calls bill to their accounts. Plaintext is encrypted at rest with envelope encryption (AES-256-GCM); the resolver falls back to platform-level env vars when no per-tenant override exists.

baseline.summary: No per-tenant credential storage. The voice agent's Twilio/Deepgram/ElevenLabs/OpenAI/Anthropic keys came from process.env only. Every tenant in a multi-tenant deploy would share the same vendor accounts — billing attribution impossible, key rotation per tenant impossible, white-glove onboarding meant editing the platform-level env.

current.summary: Shipped end-to-end. Migration 046_tenant_secrets (composite-PK (tenant_id, provider), opaque ciphertext column, JSONB metadata). TenantSecretResolver in @cernia/core with NoopSecretCipher (dev) + AesGcmSecretCipher (prod). apps/api boot now wires KyselyTenantSecretStore (secrets survive restart) + refuses to start in production when CERNIA_TENANT_SECRET_KEY/KID is unset or wrong length — TenantSecretBootError thrown loud. PUT/DELETE handlers emit structured tenant-secret.write/delete IAM audit rows. KMS rotation runbook (docs/operations/runbooks/tenant-secret-key-rotation.md) covers cadence, rehearsal in staging, multi-key decrypt window, re-encrypt job, and rollback.

history.note: Swapped apps/api boot from InMemoryTenantSecretStore → KyselyTenantSecretStore. Added prod-mode TenantSecretBootError refusing missing/short keys. Authored KMS rotation runbook with rehearse-in-staging + multi-key window + re-encrypt job.

stages.name: Deepgram STT adapter
stages.rationale: Speech-to-text is the input side of the voice loop. Deepgram is the BAA-covered default; per-tenant model + language overrides are read from tenant_secrets.metadata.

baseline.summary: @cernia/voice-agent has an ISpeechToText seam but no production adapter. All current eval/golden runs use a stubbed STT that echoes back a scripted transcript.

current.summary: DeepgramWsTransport production adapter shipped in apps/api/src/deepgram-ws-transport.ts. Uses ws npm package (already a dependency) with Authorization: Token header. Parses Results, SpeechStarted, UtteranceEnd, Error message types into DeepgramServerEvent. boot path wires DeepgramSttBackend when CERNIA_SPEECH_STT=deepgram + DEEPGRAM_API_KEY are set; falls back to stub STT with warn log otherwise. 13 new unit tests cover factory shape, JSON parsing (all event types), error resilience, and handler idempotency. api typecheck + full api test suite clean.

remaining: Run the STT-accuracy goldens nightly against the production API + execute the Deepgram BAA for PHI traffic (legal/procurement — the code path is now live-verified; see 2026-06-01 history). The live μ-law round-trip smoke test (apps/api/src/deepgram-stt.live.test.ts) covers the integration contract.

history.note: Deepgram STT VALIDATED live against the operator's real key — AND fixed a real bug that meant STT produced ZERO output in production. (1) BUG FIXED (apps/api/src/deepgram-ws-transport.ts): the ws 'message' handler gated on `typeof data === 'string'`, but the `ws` library delivers Deepgram's JSON TEXT frames as a Node Buffer (isBinary=false), so EVERY transcript/VAD/error message was silently dropped — STT never emitted a single transcript. The unit test gave false confidence because it called onJsonMessage() directly with a string, bypassing the ws delivery path. Fix: new handleSocketMessage(data, isBinary) decodes any non-binary frame to UTF-8 (Buffer | Buffer[] | ArrayBuffer | string); binary frames ignored. (2) Live proof: new apps/api/src/deepgram-stt.live.test.ts — a TTS→STT round trip in the Twilio telephony format (ElevenLabs Sarah → ulaw_8000 → DeepgramSttBackend encoding=mulaw/8000 over the real WS) — Deepgram transcribed 'the quick brown fox jumps over the lazy dog' at 0.998 confidence. Diagnosed via direct WS probes that confirmed the full backend query (nova-2-medical + redact + no_retention, both finish modes) transcribes correctly, isolating the defect to the transport's frame-type guard. (3) Regression guard: 3 new unit tests exercise handleSocketMessage with a Buffer text frame / string / binary (the path the old test missed). Gated on ELEVENLABS_API_KEY + DEEPGRAM_API_KEY (skipIf). ADR-0127: @cernia/api typecheck=0, build=0, transport+live tests 18/18, changed-file lint clean (1 pre-existing close() require-await baseline). Inbound transcription now works end-to-end on the operator's free-tier Deepgram key.

ra-stt-3 closed — STT is now per-tenant-aware. NEW apps/api/src/per-tenant-stt-router.ts (PerTenantSttRouter): wraps the boot-time STT backend and, on each openStream(), resolves PerTenantSpeechCredentials.resolveDeepgram(tenantId) (the ra-stt-2 helper over TenantSecretResolver) — when source==='tenant' it builds a lazily-cached, rotation-aware per-tenant DeepgramSttBackend so a tenant transcribes on — and is billed on — their own Deepgram account; otherwise it delegates to the platform default. Twin of the ra-tts-4 PerTenantTtsRouter / ra-tel-1 PerTenantTwilioVoiceRouter. Wired in apps/api/src/index.ts initialiseVoiceCallStack, gated on CERNIA_SPEECH_STT=deepgram + tenantSecretResolver (so a deployment with no platform Deepgram key still honours a per-tenant override). Tested vs real Postgres (:5434): apps/api/src/per-tenant-stt-router.integration.test.ts bootstraps the recovered 046_tenant_secrets.sql migration, seeds via KyselyTenantSecretStore+TenantSecretResolver — 4/4 (per-tenant isolation A≠B, platform-default fall-through, cache + rotation). Boot-time SpeechServiceFactory swap (replace the static env-var read in apps/api/src/index.ts with a per-tenant factory cache that consumes this helper) is ra-stt-3's remaining work.

ra-stt-4 closed (eval scaffolding level) — STT-accuracy goldens ship in @cernia/voice-agent-evals. packages/voice-agent-evals/src/stt/wer.ts implements canonical Word Error Rate via Levenshtein DP at word level (case-insensitive, punctuation-stripped tokenization) with full S/D/I breakdown. scoreSttAccuracy returns {passed, notes, wer, result} with a default 0.2 threshold (80% accuracy). packages/voice-agent-evals/src/stt/golden.ts: SttGolden type + runSttGolden with a pluggable Transcriber interface — the same goldens run against (a) tests/external-fakes/deepgram-fake.ts in CI, (b) real Deepgram nightly, (c) Whisper / Azure Speech. echoTranscriber + constantTranscriber for unit tests. 16 tests pass: 12 WER unit (identical text + case/punct ignored + S/D/I single-error + empty edge cases + threshold boundary) + 4 golden runner (echo passes / constant fails / custom threshold / ENOENT on missing WAV). Public barrel exports added. Real-Deepgram WAV+transcript fixture commit + nightly run is the next followup (procurement-blocked on Deepgram production key + BAA per ra-stt-2).

stages.name: ElevenLabs TTS adapter
stages.rationale: Text-to-speech is the output side of the loop. ElevenLabs is the default; per-tenant voice id is read from tenant_secrets.vendorAccountId. Tenant-defined persona (existing voice-agent persona editor) selects the voice.

baseline.summary: ITextToSpeech seam exists; no production adapter. Persona editor already exposes a voiceId picker with sample previews but every save is dropped on the floor.

current.summary: ElevenLabsFetchTransport production adapter shipped in apps/api/src/elevenlabs-fetch-transport.ts. Uses Node 22 native fetch with xi-api-key header + streaming ReadableStream body iteration. boot path wires ElevenLabsTtsBackend when CERNIA_SPEECH_TTS=elevenlabs + ELEVENLABS_API_KEY are set; falls back to stub TTS with warn log otherwise. 8 new unit tests cover factory shape, streaming multi-chunk assembly, HTTP 4xx error propagation, null body guard, and close idempotency. api typecheck clean.

history.note: ElevenLabs TTS FULLY VALIDATED live on the free tier — real μ-law-8000 phone audio confirmed end-to-end, AND a real telephony bug fixed. (1) Free-tier voice: researched + probed the operator's key — Library voices and OLD defaults (Rachel 21m00Tcm4TlvDq8ikWAM) return 402 paid_plan_required, but NEW default voices work free via API; verified HTTP 200 for Sarah (EXAVITQu4vr4xnSDxMaL, female), Laura, Lily, Jessica, Will (Aria is library-gated). Live test now defaults to Sarah. (2) BUG FIXED (packages/speech/src/backends/elevenlabs-tts-backend.ts): output_format was sent in the request BODY, which ElevenLabs's /v1/text-to-speech/:id/stream endpoint IGNORES — it silently returned its mp3_44100_128 default (observed: an 'ID3'-tagged MP3, head 49443304). For the telephony path that is catastrophic: Twilio Media Streams expect raw μ-law 8 kHz and would have received MP3 → broken call audio. Moved output_format to the query string; the live synth now returns real μ-law (head ff ff 7e … — 0xFF silence + speech samples, 16 KB, no ID3). Updated the 2 backend unit tests that asserted body.output_format → assert the query param + body absence (6/6 green). The live test's format guard now keys off the ID3 signature only (μ-law leading silence 0xFF would false-trip an MPEG frame-sync check). ADR-0127: @cernia/speech typecheck=0/lint=0/build=0/6 tests; @cernia/api typecheck=0, live test 2/2. This bug would have shipped silently — the gated live smoke test caught it. The agent now speaks real phone audio on the operator's free tier; production voice is selected per-tenant via voice_tenant_config.persona.

LIVE ElevenLabs TTS smoke test shipped + run against the operator's real API key (apps/api/src/elevenlabs-tts.live.test.ts). Gated like the Stripe live-sandbox test (describe.skipIf on ELEVENLABS_API_KEY); mirrors the telephony path exactly — constructs ElevenLabsTtsBackend (model eleven_flash_v2_5) over the production buildElevenLabsFetchTransportFactory() and synthesizes a short phrase at format 'mulaw-8k' (→ output_format ulaw_8000, what Twilio Media Streams require). Contract assertion: a correct key + request shape + endpoint yields EITHER real μ-law audio (usable voice + adequate plan) OR a clean HTTP 402 paid_plan_required; only 401/400/422/5xx fail (real drift). VERIFIED live (2/2 green): the operator's key AUTHENTICATES and has TTS access, and our request reaches the /v1/text-to-speech/:voiceId/stream endpoint correctly — proven by a clean 402, not a 401/400. FINDING: on a FREE ElevenLabs tier, the API rejects ALL stock/Library voices ('Free users cannot use library voices via the API') — confirmed for both the operator's chosen Library voice (g6xIsTj2HwM6VR4iXFCw) and the canonical default Rachel (21m00Tcm4TlvDq8ikWAM). So free-tier API TTS is unusable with stock voices; producing real μ-law audio needs a paid plan (any tier) or the tenant's own voice. The byte-level μ-law assertion (incl. an MP3-default guard against the /stream endpoint ignoring output_format) is dormant until then. CERNIA_ELEVENLABS_TEST_VOICE_ID env override lets CI point at a usable voice. The .env key + CERNIA_SPEECH_TTS=elevenlabs are set so the boot path builds the real backend. ADR-0127: apps/api typecheck=0; test file is eslint-ignored (test glob); pre-existing package-wide lint debt untouched.

Cross-pod persona persistence CLOSED (the last tts-backend followup). Admin PATCH /api/voice-agent/persona now persists to voice_tenant_config.persona (migration 066) via PostgresVoiceTenantResolver.getPersona/setPersona — the SAME jsonb column the voice-call-handler resolver reads in rowToConfig(), so a persona edit is durable + visible to every pod's resolve(), not process-local. This SUPERSEDES the original Organization.extension[voice-agent-persona] followup target: the resolver does not read Organization.extension, it reads voice_tenant_config.persona, so writing to Organization.extension would never reach the call path. setPersona does a targeted UPDATE (leaves brand_name / escalation_phone / dialog_policy untouched) and returns false → PATCH 409 {error:'no-voice-config'} when the tenant has no base voice config (brand_name + escalation_phone are NOT NULL, so a persona can't precede the base row). personaStore wired in apps/api/src/index.ts (the PostgresVoiceTenantResolver is passed as personaStore when db is present; in-memory Map fallback retained for no-db / tests). 5 new real-Postgres integration tests + 8 existing resolver tests pass (13/13) against cernia-f1-test-pg:5434; apps/api typecheck clean; 7 pre-existing carry-forward lint errors only (verified identical via git-stash baseline — my additions introduce zero new lint errors). Files: apps/api/src/postgres-voice-tenant-resolver.ts (getPersona/setPersona), apps/api/src/voice-agent-admin-routes.ts (exported PersonaStore interface + persona GET/PATCH rewired to opts.personaStore with Map fallback), apps/api/src/index.ts (personaStore wiring), apps/api/src/voice-agent-persona-persistence.integration.test.ts.

ra-tts-4 voiceId-routing followup closed — PerTenantTtsRouter now applies a per-tenant voice id override. On each synthesize() call, when the tenant secret resolved source='tenant', the router overrides TtsOpts.voiceId with the secret's metadata.defaultVoiceId (preferred) or vendorAccountId (fallback) before delegating to the per-tenant ElevenLabs backend; default fall-through preserves the caller's persona voiceId unchanged. The override is cached alongside the backend in CacheEntry.voiceIdOverride and refreshed on each resolve so an operator updating only the voice id (same API key) takes effect without a key rotation. 6 new unit tests (15 total): metadata.defaultVoiceId override, vendorAccountId fallback, metadata-preferred-over-vendorAccountId, no-override preserves caller voiceId, default-fallthrough preserves caller voiceId, same-key voice-id refresh. apps/api typecheck clean (3 pre-existing carry-forward errors only). Files: apps/api/src/per-tenant-tts-router.ts + .test.ts.

ra-tts-4 closed — PerTenantTtsRouter shipped in apps/api/src/per-tenant-tts-router.ts. Wraps the default TextToSpeech backend (built at boot from ELEVENLABS_API_KEY env var) with a tenant-aware adapter that consults PerTenantSpeechCredentials.resolveElevenLabs(tenantId) on each synthesize() call. When the resolver returns source='tenant' the router builds (or reuses a cached) ElevenLabsTtsBackend keyed on that tenant's API key and delegates to it; when source='platform-env' or no override exists, falls through to the boot default backend. Bounded LRU cache (default 64 tenants, eviction by lastUsedAt) prevents unbounded growth at scale. Each tenant-keyed call emits a speech.tts.started audit row with backend='elevenlabs-tenant' for cost-allocation. Boot wiring in apps/api/src/index.ts: initialiseVoiceCallStack() now accepts deps.tenantSecretResolver; when supplied AND the default tts.id is 'elevenlabs', the tts is wrapped before being passed to registerInboundCallHandler. 9 unit tests cover the full matrix: no override → default fallthrough, source='platform-env' → default fallthrough, source='tenant' → tenant backend used, cache reuse, secret rotation triggers rebuild, LRU eviction, audit double-emit suppression on default fallthrough, id+hasBaa report the default's (audit-field stability), invalidate() drops a cached entry. apps/api typecheck clean (only pre-existing carry-forward errors on admin-tenants-routes 'update' field, unchanged).

Voice persona PATCH endpoint shipped — apps/api/src/voice-agent-admin-routes.ts now exposes GET + PATCH /api/voice-agent/persona keyed by tenantId from rbacClaims, backed by an in-memory personaStore (single-pod safe). Replaces the console.log placeholder in apps/admin/src/app/admin/tenants/[id]/voice-agent/page.tsx handleSave — the persona editor's Save now round-trips the full VoiceAgentPersona shape (agentName, voiceId, voiceGender, voiceAccent, formality, warmth, pace, greeting/signoff templates) through real HTTP with the X-Cernia-Tenant header. Cross-pod followup: write through to Organization.extension[voice-agent-persona] via PgStorageAdapter so the voice-call-handler tenant resolver sees the update. apps/api typecheck clean.

ra-tts-2 closed (resolver wiring level) — PerTenantSpeechCredentials.resolveElevenLabs(tenantId) shipped in apps/api/src/per-tenant-speech-credentials.ts. Returns {plaintext, source, vendorAccountId, metadata} so vendorAccountId surfaces the tenant's default voiceId and metadata?.defaultVoiceId surfaces the persona editor's choice. ELEVENLABS_API_KEY env-var fallback automatic. Shared with ra-stt-2 — 10 unit tests cover the full matrix (per-tenant override / env-var fallback / tenant isolation / undefined-when-empty). apps/api typecheck clean. Boot-time SpeechServiceFactory swap (consume the helper in apps/api/src/index.ts so the static ELEVENLABS_API_KEY env-var read becomes per-call per-tenant resolution) is ra-tts-4's remaining work.
ElevenLabsFetchTransport native-fetch streaming adapter + apps/api boot wiring. 8 tests.

stages.name: Phase 1 — Real backend infrastructure
stages.rationale: Postgres + MinIO + Redis + Mailpit + NATS in Docker, apps/api boot against real DB, dev-seed produces one real tenant + users + FHIR resources, all 4 Next.js apps running natively. Required for any swap to be testable end-to-end.

baseline.summary: Apps had never been started against a real backend on this machine. Postgres data dir on OneDrive caused fsync hangs; a native PG service was bound to host:5432 colliding with the Docker stack; admin tenant routes weren't wired; api Dockerfile didn't copy scripts/ (postinstall fail); storage migrate.js couldn't find src/migrations/ (tsc doesn't copy .sql); connection-routes.ts double-registered POST /Connection over @cernia/server's generic FHIR CRUD; 122 pnpm-store packages were partially extracted.

current.summary: Full stack up: Postgres 16 on :5433 (production durability flags), MinIO :9000/:9001, Redis, Mailpit, NATS. 40 migrations applied. tenant-acme seeded (20 FHIR resources). apps/api on :8080 with real RSA jwks. Admin :3000, trust :3001, patient :3002, installer :3003 all return HTTP 200. Fixes: docker-compose.override.yml remaps PG to 5433, scripts/ copied into api Dockerfile, migrate.js resolver tries dist/migrations then src/migrations fallback, connection-routes.ts removed duplicate POST /Connection (keeps $run), fix-pnpm-store.mjs repaired 122 broken packages.

CIT harness Hypothesis B test (ra-cit-harness-1-bisect): ran a bisect test inside vitest that applied migrations one-at-a-time with stdout progress logging. Result: migrations 001-012 succeeded in vitest. The test then printed `[bisect 13/88] 013_audit_sharding.sql` and never printed 14/88 — i.e. migration 013 hung or the transition to 014 hung. The 180s testTimeout triggered after 218s wall-clock. Migration 013 contents (packages/storage/src/migrations/013_audit_sharding.sql): 6 ALTER TABLE statements on audit_log (ADD COLUMN shard_id NOT NULL DEFAULT 0, ADD CONSTRAINT CHECK shard_id >= 0, ADD COLUMN prev_shard_hash, UPDATE audit_log SET prev_shard_hash (no-op on empty table)) + 2 CREATE INDEX on audit_log + 4 ALTER TABLE resources ADD COLUMN ... GENERATED ALWAYS AS (...) STORED for SecurityFinding + 3 CREATE INDEX on resources. Standalone Node script applies all 88 migrations in ~600ms, so the issue is the vitest interaction with this specific multi-statement SQL file (whole file is one `sql.raw(text).execute(trx)` call). Next hypothesis to try: split migrations on statement boundaries before passing to sql.raw — if per-statement execution works in vitest where bulk does not, the fix is a runMigrations refactor. Bisect test (postgres-fixture-bisect.test.ts) deleted after the diagnostic; the finding is recorded inline in the remaining bullet so future agents can pick up from this point.

CIT harness Hypothesis C test: applied migrations 001-013 one SQL statement at a time inside vitest (each statement in its own sql.raw().execute(trx) call, all inside the same transaction). Hypothesis was that splitting on statement boundaries would unblock the hang isolated to migration 013 in Hypothesis B. Result: still hangs, BUT exposed a much earlier slow point — migration 010_phase2_tier3_terminology_context.sql statement #20 (ALTER TABLE resources ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS grp_actual TEXT GENERATED ALWAYS AS ...) took 8731ms (vs ~ms expected for an ALTER on an empty table), and the next statement(s) hung. Combined with Hypothesis B`s finding (whole-file 013 hangs, which also has many GENERATED-column ALTERs), the root cause class is narrowed to PostgreSQL `GENERATED ALWAYS AS (...) STORED` column DDL under vitest`s forks worker — likely an interaction between pg-driver`s simple-query protocol, vitest`s stdio/IPC channel, and Postgres`s row-recomputation phase for the new generated column. Diagnostic tests deleted after the finding was recorded; next hypotheses listed for the next agent (extended query protocol; statement_timeout to convert hang to error; vitest pool=threads instead of forks; testcontainers Postgres).
